title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
text_en: In your neck are two major blood vessels called the carotid arteries, which carry oxygen-rich blood from your heart to your brain. You can feel the pulse of the carotid artery in the side of your neck.

title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
text_en: Fatty deposits called atherosclerotic plaques can build up inside the carotid artery. These plaques can narrow the passageway of the artery and interfere with blood flow to the brain. This blockage or narrowing is called a stenosis.

title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
text_en: If blood flow to the brain is blocked temporarily, a transient ischemic attack, also known as a TIA, may occur.

title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
text_en: When the blood flow is completely obstructed, as in a carotid artery blockage, a stroke
